title: RNAi Plasmid Construction using pFGC5941
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"60lo5","text":"This protocol is based on the vector pFGC5941 (ABRC Stock CD3-447). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8ig1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3e1t9","text":"To avoid off-target effect, make sure no other regions in the interested genome perfectly match the RNAi fragment (150-500 bp) for a contiguous block longer than 16 bp. Also, make sure there are no restriction sites for the enzymes NcoI, AscI, BamHI, or XbaI within the RNAi fragment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ma5d","text":"
When designing primers to amplify the RNAi fragment. Add “GTTCTAGACCATGG” at the 5’ end of the Forward primer and add “GTGGATCCGGCGCGCC” at the 5’ end of the Reverse primer. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rhac","text":"
Make sure you have digested the pFGC5941 vector using NcoI/AscI before the first ligation. 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75d3c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8u6rj","text":"Primer sequences: 
pFGC5941_2372F: CTTCATCGAAAGGACAGTAGAA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8s2d","text":"pFGC5941_3082R: CCAAACAGGCTCATAGATACT ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6m6gj","text":"pFGC5941_3930F: TGTACATCAGAATGTTTCTGAC ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ooat","text":"pFGC5941_4430R: CGCTCTATCATAGATGTCGCTA 
 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cogfu","text":"For Safety Warnings and Hazard Information please refer to the SDS (Satety Data Sheet). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Amplify insert from cDNA or gDNA (if the fragment contains no intron) using Phusion PCR Make TWO 20 µL reactions of the following in separate tubes: %s %s
Digest one insert with NcoI/AscI and the other one insert BamHI/XbaI. See step 12 for BamHI/XbaI digestion. %s %s
First Ligation (Would like insert to vector molar ratio to 2:1 to 6:1) %s %s
Colony PCR to check for first insert %s %s
Run Colony PCR %s %s
Pick two correct colonies and inoculate into 3 mL, LB+Kan broth .
Digest plasmid with BamHI/XbaI
(d Ligadtodioo(n %s %s
Colony PCR to check for second insert. pFGC5941 3930 F & pFGC5941 4430 R Vector without insert will give a band of 500bp.
Pick two correct colonies and inoculate into 3 mL, LB+Kan broth .
Check plasmid for inserts PCR to check for both inserts: 2372F/3082R or RNAi_R (insert specific) 3930F/4430R or RNAi_F (insert specific)

Use 4 primers: 2372F, 3082R, 3930F, 4430R In the sequencing reaction, add DMSO to aid in the sequencing across the restriction enzyme digest sites (the chromatogram peaks usually drop off dramatically right after the digest sites; an alternative strategy is to PCR the final plasmid with 2372F&3082R for the left insert and 3930F&4430R for the right insert and then sequence the PCR product). Primer sequences: pFGC5941_2372F: CTTCATCGAAAGGACAGTAGAA pFGC5941_3082R: CCAAACAGGCTCATAGATACT pFGC5941_3930F: TGTACATCAGAATGTTTCTGAC pFGC5941_4430R: CGCTCTATCATAGATGTCGCTA
Transform into agrobacterium for infiltration.
Incubate samples for 01:00:00 at 37 °C .
Gel purify digests and save the BamHI/XbaI digested insert for the second ligation.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Transform 10 µL into E. coli competent cells (homemade) and plate on Kan plates.
Run Phusion PCR program: %s %s
Circle the biggest colonies on your plate and label them 1-8.
Make a replica plate for your colonies.
PCR across the first insert using primers on the vector to check for an insert: An empty vector will give a band of 700bp
Incubate in 37 °C shaker overnight .
The next day, do a plasmid prep (mini-prep kit) with 1 of the colonies that grew well.
Incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
Gel purify digest.
Incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Transform 10 µL into E.Coli competent cells (homemade) and plate on Kan plates.
Incubate in 37 °C shaker overnight.
Plasmid prep (mini-prep kit).
%s %s
Gel purify digest.
2 µL vector that contains the first insert, digested with BamHI/XbaI ( ~175 ng; adjust volume based on concentration ). %s %s
Incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Transform 10 µL into E. coli competent cells (homemade) and plate on Kan plates.
pFGC5941 3930 F & pFGC5941 4430 R Vector without insert will give a band of 500bp
Pick two correct colonies and inoculate into 3 mL, LB+Kan broth .
Incubate in 37 °C shaker overnight.
Plasmid prep (mini-prep kit)
PCR to check for both inserts: 2372F/3082R or RNAi_R (insert specific) 3930F/4430R or RNAi_F (insert specific)

Use 4 primers: 2372F, 3082R, 3930F, 4430R Note: in the sequencing reaction, add DMSO to aid in the sequencing across the restriction enzyme digest sites (the chromatogram peaks usually drop off dramatically right after the digest sites; an alternative strategy is to PCR the final plasmid with 2372F&3082R for the left insert and 3930F&4430R for the right insert and then sequence the PCR product)
Transform into agrobacterium for infiltration.
creator: Andrea Sweigart
